# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### January 17, 2017

> **⚠️ Disclaimer:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript of the board meeting. They may contain errors or omissions. For official minutes, please consult the Conroe ISD Board of Trustees.

---

## 1. Public Hearing: Texas Academic Performance Report (TAPR)

**Presenter:** Dr. Null (Academics)

Dr. Null presented the annual Texas Academic Performance Report, covering district performance, finances, and student outcomes. Key highlights:

### Academic Performance
- **2016 Accountability Rating:** Met Standard
- **2016 Special Education Determination Status:** Meets Requirements (improved from prior year where the state had flagged the district for needed improvement)
- **Enrollment:** Official enrollment was 58,014 the previous year; current enrollment as of the meeting date was **59,960**, approaching the 60,000 threshold
- **STAAR Level II (all subjects):** 85% of students met standard; 30% at advanced standard
- **Progress measures (Index 2):** 68% in math, 64% in reading
- **English Language Learners:** 61% at Level II
- **Special Education:** 49% at Level II (all subjects)
- **Economically Disadvantaged:** 73% at Level II, 13% at advanced
- **Attendance Rate:** 96.2% — improved significantly since 2011 when the district was below the state average
- **Dropout Rate:** 0.1% (grades 7–8), 0.5% (grades 9–12)
- **4-Year Graduation Rate (Class of 2015):** 95.7%; 2.1% continued to 5th year
- **5-Year Graduation Rate (Class of 2014):** 96.5%
- **Recommended/Distinguished Achievement Graduates:** 88.7%
- **Students taking AP exams:** 36.2% (noted district also has robust dual credit programs)
- **AP scores above criterion:** 66%
- **SAT/ACT participation (Class of 2015):** 67.9%

### College Readiness Data (2014 Graduates in Texas Public Colleges)
- 1,846 students tracked (roughly half of ~3,300 graduates; others in private, out-of-state, or trade schools)
- Nearly 900 had a GPA of 3.0 or above in their first year
- 382 students had GPAs below 2.0

### Financial Performance
- **Texas Smart Schools (formerly FAST) Rating:** 5-Star rating for all 6 years of the system's existence — only three districts in Texas have achieved this (Conroe ISD, Cy-Fair, and Friendswood)
- **Instruction spending:** 60.42% of operating budget (above state average; was below state average ~10–12 years ago)
- **Guidance & Counseling:** Above state average (additional counselors added in recent years, including crisis counselor)
- **Transportation:** Significantly above state average due to district size (~30,000 miles/day)
- **General Administration:** Nearly half the state average as a percentage of budget
- **Teacher average experience:** 11.37 years
- **Teacher turnover rate:** 13.7% (below state average)

### Campus Incidents
- Felony controlled substance remains the #1 reportable incident, most commonly prescription drugs from home

### Reportable Discipline Incidents
- Noted as relatively low given 60,000 students and ~10 million student-school-days per year

**Public Comment on TAPR:** No public comments were made.

---

## 2. Call to Order & Opening
- **Time:** 6:15 PM
- **Quorum confirmed**
- **Invocation:** Mr. Sanders (read the Prayer of St. Francis of Assisi)
- **Pledges of Allegiance:** Mr. Williams (Texas and U.S. flags)

---

## 3. Special Recognition: School Board Appreciation Month

**Presenter:** Bethany Medford, Principal, Bozman Intermediate School

- January is School Board Appreciation Month in Texas
- Students provided cards, posters, banners, drawings, candy, and small gifts
- Board members expressed gratitude to staff, teachers, principals, and administration
- Multiple board members commented on the quality of instruction and leadership across the district

---

## 4. Citizen Participation

### Speaker: Kellie Maxwell
- Raised concerns about **transportation for students attending CISD academies** (specifically in the Oak Ridge and Caney Creek feeder zones)
- Noted that gifted students who qualify and are invited to attend district academies face a transportation burden — families must provide their own transportation or arrange carpools
- Expressed safety concerns about informal carpooling arrangements with unscreened drivers
- Pointed out that students sent to DAEP receive district-provided busing (passing academy locations along the way)
- **Requested:** Reliable, district-provided transportation with screened drivers for academy students

---

## 5. Consent Agenda

**Vote: Approved unanimously (all in favor)**

*(No items were pulled for separate discussion.)*

---

## 6. Item 4A: Approval of 2017–2018 School Calendar

**Presenter:** Dr. Null

### Key Features of the Approved Calendar:
- **First day of school:** August 16, 2017 (Wednesday — mid-week start, popular with parents and staff)
- **First grading period ends:** October 6 (early release day), followed by October 9 school holiday
- **Thanksgiving break:** Full week off (continued tradition)
- **First semester ends:** December 20 (84 days of instruction — 5 more than current year's first semester)
- **Winter break:** December 21 – January 8; teachers return January 8, students January 9
- **Spring break:** March (with early release on March 9 — 4th early release day added per approved waiver amendment)
- **Last day for students:** May 31
- **Last day for teachers:** June 1
- **First semester: 84 days / Second semester: 94 days** (near-ideal balance when accounting for spring testing)

### Process:
- District Level Planning & Decision-Making Committee worked since October
- Started with 8 draft calendars, narrowed to 3 for public input
- Over **2,300 public comments** received via district website
- District of Innovation status allowed earlier start date and additional flexibility

**Vote: Approved unanimously (all in favor)**

---

## 7. Item 5A: Adoption of Attendance Zones for Oak Ridge Feeder Schools

**Presenter:** Dr. Hines (with Dr. Null)

### Background — New Schools Opening:
1. **Lucile J. Bradley Elementary** (K–4) — Opening August 2017, at 4200 Falls Lake Drive
2. **Flex 18 Intermediate** (Grades 5–6) — Opening August 2018, at Woodson's Reserve Parkway/French Lane (~1,000 students)
3. **Grand Oaks High School** (opening as 9–10 in August 2018) — at 4800 Riley Fuzzel Road; will phase in grades until full 9–12 by August 2020 (~3,100 student capacity)

### Attendance Boundary Committee Process:
- Committee formed in October; multiple members recognized at the meeting
- Three rounds of community forums conducted
- Over **1,000 comments** received via website
- Multiple scenarios developed and narrowed based on community feedback

### Approved Recommendations:

#### Elementary — Scenario 2:
- Falls South, Legends Trace, Rights Landing, Bristol Lakes: Birnam Woods → **Bradley**
- Creekside: Broadway → **Bradley**
- Legends Run (west of Birnam Woods Drive): Snyder → **Bradley**
- The Meadows: Birnam Woods → **Kauffman**
- Harmony (north of Lexington, east of Rayford): Broadway → **Snyder**
- No impact on Title I status for any campus

#### Intermediate — Scenario 2.1:
- All of Bradley feeds into **Flex 18 Intermediate**
- Snyder feeds into **Flex 18** (due to proximity and traffic patterns)
- All of Broadway feeds into **Cox Intermediate**
- The Meadows moves from Cox to **Vogel**
- Creekside, Falls, Benders Landing, Legends Run, Legends Trace, Rights Landing, Bristol Lakes, Woodson's Reserve: Cox → **Flex 18**
- Note: Future rezoning anticipated when Flex 19 opens in 2019 in the 242 corridor

#### High School — Scenario 1:
- Follows current junior high feeder zone pattern with one exception:
  - **The Meadows** moves from York feeder → **Irons feeder** (to maintain single feeder pattern K–12)
- Neighborhoods zoned to **Grand Oaks High School** include: The Falls, Legends Trace, Benders Landing, Legends Run, Rights Landing, Bristol Lakes, Lockridge Farms, Harmony, Woodson's Reserve, Spring Creek Pines, Discovery Creek Apartments
- **Oak Ridge High School enrollment concern acknowledged** — will be smaller than optimal
  - District plans to address by: creating an Academy program drawing from Grand Oaks; allowing transfers from over-capacity high schools; allowing transfers for JROTC program at Oak Ridge
  - Oak Ridge's central location provides long-term flexibility

### Transition Policy:
- Students in their **last year** at a school may finish there (with parent-provided transportation)
- **Meadows students** specifically offered transition option from intermediate through high school
- Students currently at Oak Ridge High School will finish there

### Board Comments:
- Board members praised the transparency of the process, the thoroughness of the committee's work, and the multiple opportunities for public input
- Acknowledged that not everyone would be pleased but expressed confidence in the quality of all schools
- Thanked parents and community members who participated

**Vote: Approved unanimously (all in favor)**

---

## 8. Item 5B: Capital Improvements Update

**Presenter:** Mr. Foster

| Project | Status |
|---------|--------|
| **Grangerland Intermediate Classroom Additions** | Complete — students using classrooms since return from winter break; closing out contract |
| **Safety & Security Phase 1** | Wrapping up January; Phase 2 to come to board in March |
| **The Woodlands College Park Robotics Addition** | On schedule for March turnover; weather-dependent (rain causing delays with roofing) |
| **Network Operations Center** | On schedule; core center in progress; turnover scheduled for July; complex migration from old to new equipment planned |
| **Lucile J. Bradley Elementary** | On schedule for August 2017 opening; exterior progressing well; interior finishing underway (library, gymnasium, light fixtures being installed) |
| **2017 Life Cycle Project** | Approved previous month; contractor kickoff complete; work beginning; update expected at February meeting |
| **Knox Science Classrooms & Woodlands Transportation Facility Expansion** | Contractors mobilized; foundations/infrastructure underway; both scheduled for August 2017 turnover |
| **Grand Oaks High School** | On schedule for August 2018 opening; building structure essentially complete; roofing progressing west to east; interior corridors, admin areas, plumbing, and infrastructure being installed |

## 9. Item 6A: Approval of 2015–2016 Comprehensive Annual Financial Report (CAFR)

**Presenter:** Mr. Darren Rice; **Auditor:** Kevin Sanford, Partner, Weaver (independent auditors)

- Finance staff recognized: Janice Towers, Cindy Westar, Karen Garza
- CAFR was reviewed by the Audit Committee and **favorably received**
- Mr. Sanford thanked the district for the opportunity to serve as auditors and recognized the extensive work by district staff

**Vote: Approved unanimously (all in favor)**
*(Motion made by Audit Committee Chair)*

---

## 10. Closed Session: Student Expulsion Appeal (Policy FOD)

- **Closed to the public** pursuant to Local Government Code §551.0821 (discipline of public school children/personally identifiable student information)
- Board entered closed session at **6:59 PM**

### Outcome:
- **Motion to deny the expulsion appeal as presented**
- **Vote: 6–0 (approved unanimously)**
- A board member made a personal comment to the student/family, encouraging the student to make the most of their time in the Juvenile Justice program (JJP), expressing belief it is one of the best such programs in the state/nation, and encouraging the student to use the experience as a learning opportunity

---

## 11. Adjournment

**Motion to adjourn: Approved**

| Item | Motion | Vote |

| 2017–2018 School Calendar | Approve as presented | **Unanimous (all in favor)** |
| Attendance Zones (Oak Ridge Feeder Schools) | Approve as recommended | **Unanimous (all in favor)** |
| 2015–2016 CAFR | Approve as presented | **Unanimous (all in favor)** |
| Student Expulsion Appeal | Deny appeal as presented | **6–0** |
| Adjournment | Motion to adjourn | **Approved** |
